honoree.name: Johnny Cash
honoree.summary: Johnny Cash was born on February 26, 1932, in Kingsland, Arkansas, and became a legendary American singer-songwriter known as the 'Man in Black.' He was famous for his deep, commanding voice and hits like 'I Walk the Line' and 'Ring of Fire.' Cash was a pivotal figure in country music, influencing multiple genres and earning inductions into the Country Music, Rock and Roll, and Gospel Halls of Fame. He died on September 12, 2003, in Nashville, Tennessee, leaving a lasting legacy in music history.

honoree.name: Levon Helm
honoree.summary: Levon Helm was an American musician and actor born on May 26, 1939, in Elaine, Arkansas. He was the drummer and vocalist for the influential rock group The Band. Helm also had a successful solo career and acted in several films. He passed away on April 19, 2012, in New York City, leaving a significant legacy in American music.
honoree.placeOfDeath: New York City, New York, United States
